# openclaw-unison

> OpenClaw plugin that gives the AI long-term memory backed by the Unison brain —
> hybrid BM25+vector search recalled before every turn, conversations captured
> automatically, and four AI tools (search, store, forget, status) for the model
> to read and write the knowledge graph directly.

**Agents: start with [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md)** — it covers installation,
authentication with a `usk_` key + `UNISON_BASE_URL`, and the write+recall loop
this plugin implements.

The plugin enforces nothing client-side; the Unison backend is the security
boundary. Auth is a machine API key (`usk_...`) via `UNISON_TOKEN` env var or
the `apiKey` config field. All brain reads/writes go to `PUT /v1/brain/doc` and
`GET /v1/brain/search`.

## Docs
- [README.md](README.md): install, setup, env vars, configuration reference, brain path routing
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): how an agent installs and uses this plugin; contributing guide
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): development workflow, conventions, PR checklist
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): reporting vulnerabilities

## API contract
- Brain write: `PUT /v1/brain/doc` — body `{ path, bodyMd, kind, title?, tags[], source? }`
- Brain search: `GET /v1/brain/search?q=<query>&k=<limit>`
- Brain status: `GET /v1/brain/status`
- Auth header: `Authorization: Bearer <usk_key>`

## Usage
```bash
# Install the plugin
openclaw plugins install @unisonlabs/openclaw-unison

# Configure (interactive)
openclaw unison setup
openclaw gateway restart

# Or set env var directly
export UNISON_TOKEN="usk_live_..."
```

## Examples
```bash
# Headless provisioning (dev / CI)
curl -X POST https://api.unisonlabs.ai/v1/auth/provision \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"you@example.com"}'
# Returns: {"apiKey":"usk_live_...","tenantId":"..."}

# Manual recall
openclaw unison search "auth decisions"

# Manual remember
openclaw unison status
```
